stemHtml: While flying at their assigned cruising level, the pilot of an aeroplane fitted with conventional flight controls experiences a stabiliser trim runaway to the full nose-down position. If the pilot applies full elevator deflection in the opposite direction, it will...

options.a: still result in a loss of altitude because the elevator will mechanically follow the stabiliser movement.
options.b: be possible to maintain altitude, because only the elevator nose-down effectiveness will be restricted.
options.c: still result in a loss of altitude because the elevator nose up effectiveness is restricted.
options.d: be possible to maintain altitude, because the full elevator control movement will still be available.
